headline: Upcoming G7 in Evian puts serious strain on France-Switzerland relations
description: The summit, scheduled to take place on the French shore of Lake Geneva from June 15 to 17, has required the involvement of Swiss authorities due to delegations transiting through Geneva Airport and a planned demonstration in the city.

headline: Nearly 900 arrested in France after PSG's Champions League win
'We'll be back next year for the third!': PSG celebrates second Champions League title with victory party and eye on more trophies
Sweden ramps up aid to households and businesses to tackle energy crisis
description: Less than four months before the legislative elections, the liberal conservative right and the far right have announced 17.3 billion kronor (approximately €1.6 billion) worth of support measures, including halving the price of tickets for public transportation.

headline: The Chinese women striving to escape societal pressures to marry and have children
description: From climbing gyms to specialty bookstores and entrepreneur clubs, several women-focused spaces have opened in China's major cities. These places of solidarity offer women ways to push back against pressures to have children in a country where feminist activism is closely monitored.

headline: Cannes rolls out the red carpet for season 4 of 'White Lotus'
description: The acclaimed HBO series, a biting satire of the wealthy, used the backdrop of the Cannes Film Festival to shoot its fourth season, set on the Côte d'Azur. The city council is counting on the global success of the show to bring significant economic benefits.

headline: Mutual mistrust persists between football and the French far right
description: The Rassemblement National has made headlines more for its criticism and racist outbursts toward the national team than for its support during major tournaments. It has also failed to secure high-profile allies within the sport.

headline: Macron hosts his last Choose France summit against a backdrop of stalled reindustrialization
After cadmium, French doctors warn of drinking water pollution, another 'major public health issue'
French museum reports theft of arty banana, part of a multimillion-dollar piece
A Trumpist makes it through to Colombia presidential runoff
Resignation of environmental figure Steven Guilbeault weakens Canada PM Carney
Putin's war fuels the growing role of Russia's FSB intelligence agency
Simone Bellotti brings a poetic restraint to Jil Sanders
Aya Nakamura returns to the stage to claim her crown as the queen of the Stade de France
Outgoing chair Powell delivers defense of Fed independence
Gaza, Iran, Mali: The dangers of the world's rapidly shrinking media access
Is it ever a good idea to... wear a short-sleeved shirt?
Paul McCartney blends rock and melancholic ballads in 'The Boys of Dungeon Lane,' his 20th solo album
The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
description: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

headline: Sheikha Al-Mayassa Al Thani, the princess spearheading Qatar's artistic ambitions
description: A central figure in the country's cultural policy, the emir's sister has skillfully used the acquisition of prestigious works of art, the hosting of fairs and the creation of museums to build valuable diplomatic ties.

headline: 'Do you realize that your statements are completely absurd?': Inside the arrest of the Louvre burglars
description: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.
byline: Jean-Michel Décugis,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Nicolas-Charles Torrent

headline: 'Trump and Netanyahu have turned their own blindness into a weapon of mass destruction'
